metrics.label: Overall Accuracy
metrics.threshold_note: GapSight default, not a regulatory requirement
metrics.evidence_required: Test set results with accuracy score and test set description
metrics.tooltip: Proportion of correct predictions over total predictions.

remediation.what: Improve model accuracy through better data, features, or model architecture.
how: Run k-fold cross-validation to identify high-variance splits and diagnose overfitting or data quality issues.
Apply data augmentation or collect additional representative training samples for underperforming segments.
Experiment with ensemble methods (Random Forest, Gradient Boosting) or hyperparameter tuning via grid/random search.
remediation.documentation_required: Updated test report with accuracy scores, cross-validation results, and description of changes made.

metrics.label: F1 Score (weighted)
metrics.threshold_note: GapSight default, not a regulatory requirement
metrics.evidence_required: Test report including F1 score by class and overall
metrics.tooltip: Harmonic mean of precision and recall. Especially relevant for imbalanced datasets.

remediation.what: Address class imbalance and improve per-class precision/recall balance.
how: Analyze per-class precision and recall to identify which classes underperform.
Apply SMOTE oversampling or class weights in your model training to handle imbalanced data.
Re-evaluate with stratified cross-validation to confirm improvement is robust.
remediation.documentation_required: Per-class F1 scores before and after remediation, description of balancing technique applied.

metrics.label: AUC-ROC
metrics.threshold_note: GapSight default, not a regulatory requirement

framework_mappings.description: Metrics for accuracy and robustness in technical documentation

metrics.evidence_required: ROC curve and AUC value in test report
metrics.tooltip: Area under the receiver operating characteristic curve. Measures discriminative ability.

remediation.what: Improve model discrimination through threshold tuning and probability calibration.
how: Plot the ROC curve to identify the optimal operating point for your use case.
Apply probability calibration (Platt scaling or isotonic regression) to improve score reliability.
Tune the decision threshold to balance sensitivity and specificity for your deployment context.
remediation.documentation_required: ROC curve plot, selected threshold justification, calibration report.

metrics.label: Demographic Parity Difference
metrics.threshold_note: GapSight default, not a regulatory requirement

framework_mappings.description: Bias detection and mitigation in training data

metrics.evidence_required: Fairness audit report showing demographic parity difference per protected group
metrics.tooltip: Difference in positive prediction rates between protected and reference groups. Closer to 0 is better.

remediation.what: Reduce demographic parity difference using reweighing or adversarial debiasing techniques.
how: Use Fairlearn's ExponentiatedGradient with DemographicParity constraint to retrain with fairness constraints.
Apply pre-processing reweighing to adjust sample weights before training.
Evaluate tradeoffs between accuracy and fairness using Fairlearn's dashboard.
remediation.documentation_required: Fairness metrics before and after mitigation, description of protected attributes tested, mitigation method used.

metrics.label: Equalized Odds Difference
metrics.threshold_note: GapSight default, not a regulatory requirement

framework_mappings.description: Bias detection and group-specific performance reporting
Equalized odds as fairness evaluation metric

framework_mappings.reference: Annex A.6.4, Annex A.5.4, Annex A.5.5
framework_mappings.description: Bias testing and fairness KPI monitoring

metrics.evidence_required: Fairness report showing equalized odds per protected group
metrics.tooltip: Difference in true positive and false positive rates between groups. Closer to 0 is better.

remediation.what: Equalize error rates across groups using post-processing threshold optimization.
how: Use Fairlearn's ThresholdOptimizer to find group-specific decision thresholds that equalize odds.
If threshold tuning is insufficient, retrain with EqualizedOdds constraint via ExponentiatedGradient.
Document the accuracy-fairness tradeoff for each approach tested.
remediation.documentation_required: Equalized odds metrics before and after per group, threshold values or constraint parameters used.

metrics.label: Disparate Impact Ratio
metrics.threshold_note: GapSight default, not a regulatory requirement
metrics.evidence_required: Disparate impact analysis per protected group
metrics.tooltip: Ratio of positive prediction rates between protected and reference groups. Values closer to 1.0 indicate parity.
metrics.calculation_guide: fairlearn.metrics.selection_rate(y_pred, sensitive_features=group) - compute ratio between groups

remediation.what: Bring disparate impact ratio closer to 1.0 by removing discriminatory patterns from data or predictions.
how: Use AIF360's DisparateImpactRemover to transform features and reduce proxy discrimination.
Apply in-processing techniques such as adversarial debiasing during model training.
Validate improvement with four-fifths rule (ratio >= 0.80) across all protected groups.
remediation.documentation_required: Disparate impact ratios per group before and after, method applied, four-fifths rule assessment.

metrics.label: Data Drift Score
metrics.threshold_note: GapSight default, not a regulatory requirement

framework_mappings.description: Deployment and operation controls for drift

metrics.evidence_required: Drift monitoring logs and alert thresholds
metrics.tooltip: Statistical distance between training and live data distributions. Lower means less drift.

remediation.what: Reduce data drift by retraining on recent data or updating preprocessing pipelines.
how: Set up continuous data drift monitoring using statistical tests (KS test, PSI) on incoming data vs. training distribution.
Retrain the model on a recent representative data sample that captures current distribution shifts.
Update feature preprocessing pipelines to handle new data patterns and validate with A/B testing.
remediation.documentation_required: Drift monitoring logs, retraining report with before/after distribution comparisons, and updated model card.

metrics.label: Concept Drift Score
metrics.threshold_note: GapSight default, not a regulatory requirement

framework_mappings.description: Prevention of biased feedback loops and lifecycle consistency
Emergent risk tracking including concept drift

metrics.evidence_required: Concept drift monitoring logs and retraining procedures
metrics.tooltip: Measure of change in the relationship between input features and target labels over time.
metrics.calculation_guide: Compare model performance on rolling windows or use ADWIN/DDM detectors

remediation.what: Address concept drift by retraining with updated labels and validating target-feature relationships.
how: Monitor model performance on rolling time windows to detect degradation indicating concept drift.
Retrain the model with recently labeled data that reflects the current relationship between features and targets.
Implement ADWIN or DDM drift detectors in your inference pipeline for automated concept drift alerts.
remediation.documentation_required: Concept drift detection logs, retraining report with performance comparison, and updated monitoring thresholds.

metrics.label: Adversarial Robustness Score
metrics.threshold_note: GapSight default, not a regulatory requirement

framework_mappings.description: Robustness testing in verification and validation

metrics.evidence_required: Adversarial robustness test report with attack scenarios and results
metrics.tooltip: Model accuracy under adversarial attack conditions (e.g., FGSM, PGD). Higher is better.

remediation.what: Improve adversarial robustness through adversarial training and input validation.
how: Generate adversarial examples using FGSM, PGD, or AutoAttack and include them in the training set (adversarial training).
Apply input preprocessing defenses such as feature squeezing, spatial smoothing, or JPEG compression.
Implement runtime adversarial detection to flag suspicious inputs before they reach the model.
remediation.documentation_required: Adversarial robustness test report with attack scenarios, defense methods applied, and before/after accuracy under attack.

metrics.label: Explanation Coverage (SHAP/LIME)
metrics.threshold_note: GapSight default, not a regulatory requirement

framework_mappings.description: Transparency and documentation of model logic

metrics.evidence_required: Model card with SHAP values or feature importance documentation
metrics.tooltip: Proportion of model predictions for which valid SHAP or feature importance explanations are available.
metrics.calculation_guide: shap.Explainer(model) - compute coverage as explained/total predictions

remediation.what: Add SHAP or LIME explanations to increase prediction explainability coverage.
how: Integrate SHAP TreeExplainer (tree models) or KernelExplainer (any model) to generate feature importance for each prediction.
For models where SHAP is too slow, use LIME as a lightweight local explanation alternative.
Create a model card documenting global feature importance, sample local explanations, and coverage metrics.
remediation.documentation_required: Model card with SHAP/LIME values, feature importance rankings, and explanation coverage percentage.

process_requirements.label: Risk Management System
process_requirements.evidence_required: Risk register, impact assessment reports, hazard analysis, mitigation measures
process_requirements.if_no_guidance: Use ISO 31000 or NIST AI RMF templates. Estimated effort: 2-4 business days.

remediation.what: Establish a formal risk management system covering AI-specific risks.
how: Use ISO 31000 or NIST AI RMF as a template to create a risk register with identified AI risks, likelihood, and impact.
Conduct a risk assessment workshop with technical and business stakeholders to identify AI-specific hazards.
Define mitigation measures for each identified risk and assign owners with review timelines.
remediation.documentation_required: Risk register, impact assessment reports, mitigation plan with assigned owners.

process_requirements.label: AI Governance Policy
process_requirements.evidence_required: Signed AI policy document, role assignments, accountability framework
process_requirements.if_no_guidance: Document covering fairness, transparency, human oversight, and safety. Approved by leadership.

remediation.what: Draft and adopt a formal AI governance policy approved by leadership.
how: Use sample AI policy templates from OECD or IBM as a starting point.
Cover key principles: fairness, transparency, accountability, human oversight, safety, and privacy.
Obtain sign-off from executive leadership and assign a responsible AI governance officer.
remediation.documentation_required: Signed AI governance policy document, role assignments, and accountability framework.

process_requirements.label: Technical Documentation (Annex IV)
process_requirements.evidence_required: Completed Annex IV document, model cards, data lineage records, architecture diagrams
process_requirements.if_no_guidance: Use the 15-element Annex IV checklist to identify gaps.

remediation.what: Complete technical documentation covering all Annex IV elements.
how: Work through the 15-element Annex IV checklist systematically, documenting each element.
Use Google's Model Card template to structure model documentation including intended use, performance, and limitations.
Create data lineage records and architecture diagrams to complement the written documentation.
remediation.documentation_required: Completed Annex IV document, model cards, data lineage records, and architecture diagrams.

process_requirements.label: Data Governance Policy
process_requirements.evidence_required: Data governance policy, data lineage records, bias mitigation procedures
process_requirements.if_no_guidance: Document covering data collection, quality, provenance, preparation, and bias mitigation.

remediation.what: Establish a data governance policy covering collection, quality, provenance, and bias mitigation.
how: Use the DAMA-DMBOK framework as a reference for structuring data governance across the AI lifecycle.
Document data sources, collection methods, quality checks, and preprocessing steps for all training data.
Define bias detection and mitigation procedures for training data and establish data quality KPIs.
remediation.documentation_required: Data governance policy, data lineage records, data quality reports, and bias mitigation procedures.

process_requirements.label: Automated Logging and Record Keeping
process_requirements.evidence_required: Logging architecture, stored logs, incident records
process_requirements.if_no_guidance: Critical for post-deployment high-risk systems. EU AI Act Article 12 mandatory.

remediation.what: Implement automated logging for model inputs, outputs, and system events.
how: Set up MLflow or Weights & Biases to log model experiments, parameters, metrics, and artifacts.
Implement inference logging to capture model inputs, outputs, and confidence scores in production.
Configure log retention policies and access controls aligned with regulatory requirements.
remediation.documentation_required: Logging architecture documentation, log retention policy, and sample log outputs.

process_requirements.label: Quality Management System
process_requirements.evidence_required: Quality management manual, internal audit reports, corrective action logs
process_requirements.if_no_guidance: Note: micro-enterprises may use a simplified QMS per EU AI Act SME provisions.

remediation.what: Establish a quality management system covering AI development and operations.
how: Use a simplified ISO 9001 template adapted for AI systems as the foundation for your QMS.
Define processes for internal audits, corrective actions, and continuous improvement specific to AI systems.
Document testing procedures, acceptance criteria, and change management processes for model updates.
remediation.documentation_required: Quality management manual, internal audit schedule, corrective action logs, and change management records.

process_requirements.label: Third-Party and Supply Chain Management
process_requirements.evidence_required: Vendor assessments, contracts with AI responsibilities, supplier performance reviews
process_requirements.if_no_guidance: Assess third-party AI systems, pre-trained models, and data suppliers.

remediation.what: Establish a third-party and supply chain assessment process for AI components.
how: Create a vendor assessment questionnaire covering AI-specific risks: data provenance, model transparency, bias testing, and security.
Review contracts with third-party AI providers to ensure responsibilities for monitoring, updates, and incident reporting are defined.
Maintain an inventory of all third-party AI components (pre-trained models, APIs, datasets) with risk ratings.
remediation.documentation_required: Vendor assessment reports, AI component inventory, contracts with AI-specific clauses.

gpai_copyright_checklist.label: List of training data sources with licenses
Policy on AI-generated content and copyright liability

cross_metric_rules.message: High accuracy with significant fairness gap detected. EU AI Act Article 10 and NIST MEASURE 2.11 require explicit analysis and documentation of this tradeoff.
Robustness measured during development does not guarantee runtime robustness without active production monitoring.
High drift score combined with stale model requires immediate retraining plan.
Bias detected without applied mitigation. Direct EU AI Act Article 10 violation.
Absence of explainability combined with weak human oversight increases automation bias risk.
GPAI systemic risk threshold exceeded. Notification to EU AI Office is mandatory immediately.
EU AI Act Article 12 requires automated logging for high-risk systems in production.

CRITICAL.condition: Any CRITICAL_FAIL status in required metrics
CRITICAL.message: Deployment is not permitted until critical issues are resolved.

HIGH.condition: Fail rate >= 30% of required metrics OR >= 2 cross-metric CRITICAL warnings
HIGH.message: Significant gaps identified. Address before deployment. Consult a qualified legal professional.

MEDIUM.condition: Fail rate < 30% AND review rate >= 20% of required metrics
MEDIUM.message: Some areas require attention. A review with a qualified professional is recommended.

LOW.message: Profile shows a good baseline. Periodic reassessment is recommended.
